key_insight: A locking-free mixed finite element formulation using only pseudostress provides an accurate way to solve elasticity eigenproblems without enforcing stress symmetry.
engineering_relevance: This approach supports reliable eigenanalysis for solid mechanics models in 2D and 3D while reducing locking issues that can degrade standard low-order FEM results.
method: Formulate the elasticity eigenproblem purely in terms of the pseudostress tensor.
Discretize the H(div) space with tensorial standard finite element families.
Recover displacement through a postprocessing step and assess errors with a posteriori indicators.
evidence_signal: The paper reports convergence, a priori error estimates, and reliable and efficient a posteriori analysis, validated on multiple geometries and physical setups.
watch_item: The method depends on a postprocessing step for displacement recovery, so implementation quality and mesh design may affect practical accuracy.
paper_title: A locking free mixed FEM based on a pure pseudostress based formulation for the elasticity eigenproblem
